quote: Substantial deterioration with Brian experiencing some breathing distress, asking for help
Substantial deterioration with Brian experiencing some breathing distress, asking for help
Was normal earlier today without evidence of delirium, now fixated with some mild hyperactivity
Has also used 4x PRN hydromorphone last 24 hours at 1.5mg
Cognition: responding to voice, alert at times
Pain Management: s/d commenced appears settled
Breathing remains on cont O2 at 2 LPM via n/p, SOB on exertion
Family/Carer: requires support and tlc, distressed at times due to current situation
Appears settled. Nil tenderness to left hip/flank when touching. Breakthrough morphine effect. Patient appears settled and sleeping at time of report.
Appears settled. Nil tenderness to left hip/flank when touching. Breakthrough morphine effect. Patient appears settled and sleeping at time of report.
Nil tenderness to left hip/flank when touching
Breakthrough morphine effect
She was frustrated with not being able to verbalise what she wanted, which was for her knees to be up. We got it in the end with slight nods or shaking the head.
She pointed/rubbed her throat and confirmed she was sore.
She had two small periods of agitation, both due to discomfort.
Mum had a mostly restful day.
She had tears in her eyes and looked sad.
I offered a wet swab for her to suck and it helped a lot.
RASS-PAL score = -5 (Not Rousable). No response to voice or stimulation by light touch.
Thurid appears comfortable.
Moist breathing continues however does not appear bothersome.
Periods of apnoea increasing.
Patient is in terminal stage, unconscious
Son and daughter in law is staying tonight in bed with patient
Patient does not require breakthrough in this shift, she has been settled.
RASS form for trial is completed in this shift, family are given supports reassurance by staffs.
Shaking rapidly for 5 minutes, legs and body.
At 2:50pm, a dizzy spell for about 3 minutes.
Cognition: non verbal
Family in attendance at all times. Support given.
Family in attendance at all times. Support given.
Breathing irregular and shallow at times, nil signs of distress. Spontaneous on room air.
Eyes open to gentle touch, however eyes closed quickly back. Pt not engaging with staff verbally.
Pt appeared comfortable and settled. Nil breakthrough required at time of report. Nil facial grimacing, nil signs of pain or agitation. Pt settled.
Wife remained by the bedside. Nil concerns voiced.
Rousable to voice and/or touch. Able to answer short yes/no questions only.
Able to answer short yes/no questions only. Occasional whispering of words.
Resting comfortably in bed ATOR.
Wife visiting ATOR.
Pt agitated at beginning of shift e.g. pulling at catheter, pushing gown down when washed, flicking arms around.
RN notified with 2.5mg of Midazolam and 5mg of Dexmedetomidine given at approx 0745hrs with nil effect.
Very SOB on exertion. When returning to bed staff placed PRN O2 running at 2L via NP, breathing settled at rest, nil signs of respiratory distress.
Pt alert, at times answering questions appropriately.
Pt orientated to person, not orientated to place or time. Pt pleasantly confused, cooperative with care.
Family present with pt at bedside throughout shift, wife staying overnight.
Pt c/o pain, at 1800 5mg of morphine was administered via SCI by RN S, to good effect, pt settled.
Pt in good spirits this evening, happy to see all their family.
Pt unresponsive to verbal stimuli or when repositioned in bed.
Breathing very shallow with long periods of apnoea.
Nil objective signs of pain observed.
Settled evening. Nil distress observed.
Visited by wife. Wife has left ward to go home. Would like to be contacted if any changes in pt's condition.
Remains unresponsive.
Has had long periods of apnoea. Nil signs of respiratory distress.
Nil breakthroughs required. Nil signs of pain or distress.
No visitors overnight.
Pt c/o pain waving hand over throat and stating she feels terrible.
Pt c/o pain waving hand over throat and stating she feels terrible.
5mg subcut morphine given await effect.
Nil response to verbal stimuli.
Denis resting comfortably. No breakthroughs required.
Martha present.
Seems to be at ease.
Sleeping soundly, RASS -5.
Some intermittent rousability reported by family with eye flickering to conversation which they find comforting.
Report they are very happy with his care and that they feel he has been majority comfortable.
PRNs last 24hr: Hydromorphone 0.5mg, Clonazepam 2mg SC, Glycopyrronium 400mcg.
They feel he has been majority comfortable.
Speaking in small sentences.
Pt alert orientated to time place and person and cooperative with care.
Nil signs of respiratory distress. Nil complaints of pain and nil signs of pain this shift.
Pt visited by family and cats this shift.
Pt remains in good spirits, nil concerns ATOR.
No longer orientated to time or place.
Sleeping a lot more tonight. More drowsy.
Sleeping on and off but easy rousable.
Family in with pt and staying overnight.
Very settled evening.
Resting comfortably at time of report.
unsettled throughout bed wash, grimacing and not comfortable
Morphine 2.5mg subcut and Midazolam 2.5mg subcut
Marley staying overnight
Some anxiety overnight potentially exacerbated by familial stress
Settled at time of call
lorazepam x 1, DXM x 2 overnight
Responding well to dexmed when given
His drowsiness is likely multifactorial from his cancer, as well as having had temazepam and haloperidol (as well as endep and gabapentin and tapentadol)

We are unsure about the jerking however, he does have renal failure and may need switch to hydromorphone from tapentadol, and is taking gabapentin, citalopram and endep which can be associated with myoclonus
I have updated daughter Gaylene about likely deterioration and complex symptomatology. She understands that her father is declining. Wants him to be pain free and comfortable
Becoming restless and on asking states has pain
Becoming restless and on asking states has pain
given 0.5mgs of hydromorphone sc
still confused when waking, but a bit better
mostly sleeping
Hot and removed bedding, turned down heater
Hot and removed bedding, turned down heater
heard moaning informed staff he had pain to back
administered 5mg Morphine sub cut
Asking staff what do I have to do
staff reassured him he was ok and safe
has been very vague when awake and communicating little with staff
has not required any further pain relief
Shirley's husband has stayed the night
fatigued at times and sleeping on and off
can be vague at times. mostly orientated
family at bedside tonight
Looks comfortable at time of report
extremely moist chest - too low to suction - irregular deep breathing
alert - eyes open, able to nod and shake head to answer question
shaking head for no
rubbing hands together, removing sheets and blankets, arms in the air at times
400mcg glycopyrrolate given, 30mcg dexmedetomidine given, 10mg subcut morphine given
Family in attendance
Has been very settled
Alert: Phase 4
continued to become extremely distressed and agitated. calling out and screaming. pt was distress by seeing things in his room pt seeing a little girl in his room and saying he was going to drown
continued to become extremely distressed and agitated. calling out and screaming. pt was distress by seeing things in his room pt seeing a little girl in his room and saying he was going to drown
2.5mg of subcut midazolam
pt trying to climb out of bed - could hear family trying to talk to pt from nurses station
pt trying to climb out of bed - could hear family trying to talk to pt from nurses station
staff went into help
talked with pt - pt in pain
5mg subcut morphine given
Deterioration - started trial in midazolam arm
moved to single room
Deterioration - started trial in midazolam arm
moved to single room
alert to voice at times
plucking air/ agitated intervention required, very unsettled plucking the air moaning
10mg of s/c morphine and 2.5mg s/c midazolam given at 16.55hrs with effect
has become more settled towards end of evening
unconscious
Appears comfortable at time of report
Wife and friend at bedside this evening
pleasant interactions with staff
daughter in to visit - pt wife staying overnight
settled no issues voiced
family is present able to pass on his need, Son staying tonight
Patient was alert on the early shift but has been unresponsive in the evening
Intermittently waking for short periods
mostly regulated breathing, some deeper exhalations
Grimacing to light touch on hands and voice, Did not spontaneously resolve - suspect underlying pain
Outside of grimacing appears very comfortable
squeezing sensation which has been a feature of her pericardial effusion with chest pain
particularly distressed overnight. Fear behaviour w/r/t dark?
Family in attendance
happy with Heather's comfort at the moment
Orientated: At times. Confusion/Changed Behaviour: some confusion
oral medications with held this morning due to Hugh having difficulties with swallowing tablets
Restlessness when awake
When sleeping, very comfortable, raising eyebrows now and then
raising and lowering of eyebrows
Marley expressed concern her mother was unsettled and frowning. felt Kay had woken in a panic but had since settled
when asked if she was fearful she nodded her head
staff advised Marley they would ring Dr T and update him on pt current status and obtain further orders if need be
staff in agreement during time in room Kay appeared to be comfortable and showed nil signs of any distress
confused overnight and constantly trying to climb out of bed
pleasant interactions with staff
